title: Admin User Management and Transaction Review
description: The system shall allow an authenticated admin to view flagged transactions, lock or unlock user accounts, and access transaction history for any user through a dedicated interface.
rationale: To enable admins to effectively manage user accounts and ensure security by reviewing flagged transactions and controlling access as needed.
business_value: Improves the security of the system by allowing timely action on suspicious activities and maintaining compliance with security policies.

source.description: As a admin, I need to review flagged transactions, lock or unlock user accounts, and view transaction history for any user so that I can manage user accounts and maintain security.

acceptance_criteria: I can see transaction history for any user
preconditions: I am authenticated as an admin
The system has access to the user management interface
postconditions: Flagged transactions are reviewed by the admin
User account status is updated based on admin action
Transaction history is accessible for any user
functional_constraints: Transactions must be accurately flagged and reviewed
Account lock/unlock actions must be reversible
Transaction history retrieval must be fast and accurate
security_requirements: Authentication mechanism to verify admin identity
Access control to restrict access to the user management interface to only admins
Data encryption for transaction history and account information

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for viewing flagged transactions, locking/unlocking accounts, and accessing transaction history must be under 2 seconds
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle at least 100 concurrent admin users without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and actionable, interface should be intuitive and consistent

edge_cases: What happens if an admin attempts to lock/unlock a non-existent user account?
How does the system handle concurrent locking/unlocking requests from different admins?
What if there is a failure in the transaction history retrieval process?

interfaces.name: Admin API
interfaces.description: The system will provide an API for admins to interact with the user management and transaction review functionalities.

interfaces.name: User Database
interfaces.description: The system will interact with a database to retrieve, update, and manage user account information and transaction history.

test_cases.description: Admin attempts to view flagged transactions.
test_cases.expected_result: The admin should be able to see a list of all flagged transactions with details such as user ID, transaction amount, and timestamp.
The user's account should be locked, preventing further transactions, and the admin should receive confirmation of the action.

test_cases.description: Admin unlocks a previously locked user account.
test_cases.expected_result: The user's account should be unlocked, allowing transactions again, and the admin should receive confirmation of the action.

test_cases.description: Admin attempts to access transaction history for a specific user.
test_cases.expected_result: The admin should be able to see the complete transaction history for the specified user, including details such as date, amount, and type.
